claim: JetBrains Junie is fundamentally baked into language-specific IDEs (PyCharm, IntelliJ, WebStorm) unlike generic plugins
actionable: Use Junie if you work in JetBrains IDEs; language-specific scaffolding provides better context than generic AI plugins

claim: Ask mode lets developers propose and discuss changes without code generation; plan-act-verify loop replaces immediate generation
actionable: Use ask mode to understand existing codebases and propose changes before execution; shift from vibe coding to deliberate design

claim: Junie generates project-specific guidelines from codebase scan; stores them as markdown rules for consistent agent behavior
actionable: Generate and maintain project guidelines files to give coding agents clear context of standards, frameworks, and patterns

claim: Clear diff viewing and rollback capabilities let developers review every change; sign commits and manage git flow directly
actionable: Require clear diffs and rollback options in coding agent workflows; maintain control through version control

claim: Frequency of agent questions is configurable; pairs programming feel requires more human-in-the-loop approvals than vibe coding
actionable: Configure approval frequency and question cadence to match your risk tolerance and domain requirements

claim: MCP support and model switching (GPT-5, Sonnet 4.5) allow tool expansion and cost optimization per task
actionable: Use model switching to choose cheaper models for routine tasks and stronger models for complex reasoning
